steps.title: Read the actual point
steps.content: At $x=1$, the plotted data point is about $(1,12)$. That is the actual $y$-value.

steps.title: Model the line of best fit
steps.content: For the predicted value, use the line, not the dot. Two readable points on the line are about $(0,12)$ and $(5,6)$, so a quick Desmos regression gives $y\approx -1.2x+12$.

steps.title: Compare actual and predicted
steps.content: At $x=1$, the line predicts $y=-1.2(1)+12=10.8$, about $11$. The vertical difference is about $12-11=1$, so choice A is correct.
